          APPENDIX II                                                               
                                                                                    
                        Orkokisak (Western Atakapa) - English Vocabulary            
                                                                                    
           This vocabulary is taken from the publication of de Villiers du Terrage and Rivet (1919:435-36)
                                                                                    
          of the vocabulary gathered by Jean Bérenger in 1721 from nine people captured from near
                                                                                    
          Galveston Bay. Following Swanton, this vocabulary is also called Orkokisak (Akokisa), even
                                                                                    
          though it is not certain that the tribal affiliation of these speakers was indeed Orkokisak, as their
                                                                                    
          tribal name is not given in the sources used by de Villiers du Terrage and Rivet. Each Western
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          Atakapa vocabulary item is followed by any comparable word in either Yukhíti (Y) or Hiyékiti
                                                                                    
          (H), the original orthography and the original French gloss.              
                                                                                    
          an n ear [Y án; H. an] (anar) Oreille                                     
                                                                                    
          *es iv to be large [Y hec ‘to be large]                                   
                                                                                    
           kates n toe [Y hiká·t héc ‘big toe’] (quates) orteil                     
                                                                                    
           ošes n thumb [Y wó·š héc] (auxest) pouce                                 
                                                                                    
          est n shoulder (est) épaule                                               
                                                                                    
          *icon iv to be small [Y hicón ‘to be small]                               
                                                                                    
           išicon n star [Y hicón ‘to be small;’ c.f. Y kakáw hicón ‘star,’ lit. ‘small sun’
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
           (ichetions) étoiles                                                      
                                                                                    
          ikat n foot [Y hika·t] (icac) pied                                        
                                                                                    
           kates n toe [Y hiká·t héc ‘big toe’] (quates) orteil                     
                                                                                    
          iš n sun [H iš ‘star’] (iche) soleil                                      
                                                                                    
           išicon n star [Y hicón ‘to be small;’ c.f. Y kakáw hicón ‘star,’ lit. ‘small sun’
                                                                                    
           (ichetions) étoiles                                                      
                                                                                    
          it n wind [Y hi] (iit) vent                                               
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                          472                                       
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          iƛ n day [Y yíl; H i·ƛ] (idle) jour                                       
                                                                                    
          kams n penis [c.f. Y kámkamš ‘to stick out’] (cams) organs génitaux       
                                                                                    
          kat n mouth [Y kát; H kat] (cat) bouche                                   
                                                                                    
          kates n toe [Y hiká·t héc ‘big toe’] (quates) orteil                      
                                                                                    
          keš n hair [Y te·š; H taheš] (queche) cheveux                             
                                                                                    
          koko n water; sea [Y kawkáw ‘water; rain;’ H kawkaw ‘rain’] (cacaux; cocaue) eau;
                                                                                    
           mer (cf. Caddo kú·kuʔ ‘water’)                                           
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          kosm n knife [cf. Y kocnán ‘knife;’ koc ‘to cut’] (cosme) couteau         
                                                                                    
          koy n neck [Y koy ‘throat;’ H koywak ‘neck’] (coé) cou                    
                                                                                    
          maƛ n thigh [Y mál; H ma·l] (motles) cuisse                               
                                                                                    
          nilto n ship [c.f. Y tú ‘boat’] (niltax) navire                           
                                                                                    
          nok n arm [Y nók; H nok] (noc) bras                                       
                                                                                    
          oc n nose [Y úc; H ict] (audse) nez                                       
                                                                                    
          ok n rope [Y ó·] (ocq) cordage                                            
                                                                                    
           oketa n blanket [c.f. Y ók ‘cloth’] (oqueta) couverture                  
                                                                                    
            oketasen n breeches (oquetasenne) culotte                               
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
           okmeƛ n black blanket [c.f. Y ók ‘cloth;’ mé·l ‘to be black’] (ocqmesle) couverture
                                                                                    
            blanche ou noire                                                        
                                                                                    
          os n tooth [Y óc; H oc] (hos) dent                                        
                                                                                    
          ošes n thumb [Y wó·š héc] (auxest) pouce                                  
                                                                                    
          ošpa n hand [Y wó·š ‘hand;’ wó·špe ‘ten;’ H wiš] (auchepa) main           
                                                                                    
          oƛ n eye [Y wó·l; H wil] (audle) oeil                                     
                                                                                    
          oyt iv to sleep [Y óy; H oy] (oyte) dormir                                
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                          473                                       
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          pemur ‘they shoot’ [c.f. Y pém ‘to shoot’] (pemur) canon                  
                                                                                    
          saš n head [Y iša·t; H ašat] (sache) tête                                 
                                                                                    
          seksa n elbow [Y nokšúk] (secsa) coude                                    
                                                                                    
          semak n finger [c.f. Y wó·š mák ‘fist’] (semacq) doigt                    
                                                                                    
          šak n man [Y išá·k] (chaque) homme

          APPENDIX III                                                              
                                                                                    
          Yukhíti kinship terminology                                               
                                                                                    
          Note: These terms were collected from female speakers only.               
                                                                                    
          Second ascending generation:                                              
                                                                                    
          kulšá ‘grandfather’ nilá ‘grandmother’                                    
                                                                                    
          First ascending generation:                                               
                                                                                    
          itéˑt ‘father’ okéˑt ‘mother’ kelé·t / hile·t ‘aunt, maternal or paternal’ wáhš ‘uncle, maternal or
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          paternal’ hišáŋ ‘mother-in-law’ hišáŋ šakyól ‘father-in-law’              
                                                                                    
          Self’s generation                                                         
                                                                                    
          self, female: išn̥ céˑt ‘brother’ išn̥ céˑt hicón ‘younger brother’ išn̥ céˑt hiláy ‘sister-in-law’ lé·t
                                                                                    
          ‘sister’ hišileˑt ‘younger sister’ išiwéˑt ‘elder sister’ wéˑt ‘sister or female cousin’ ipén / ipénta
                                                                                    
          ‘male cousin’ lé·tta ‘female cousin’ hipá ‘husband’                       
                                                                                    
          self, male: n̥ céˑt ‘brother (?)’ iškišéˑt ‘sister’ hiláy ‘wife’          
                                                                                    
          First descending generation                                               
                                                                                    
          iškišíl ‘daughter’ hiyóˑl ‘son’ hiyó·l yúc ‘elder son’ hiyo·l hicón ‘younger son’ nómš ‘child’ téns
                                                                                    
          ‘nephew’ tensá ‘niece’                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          Second descending generation                                              
                                                                                    
          níl ‘grandchild; granddaughter(?)’ kúlš (?) ‘grandson’                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                          475

          accent, pitch levels, 25-28
